name: MQTT Monitor
description: Subscribe to an MQTT broker topic and trigger jobs when messages are received — ideal for IoT telemetry and device event pipelines

triggers.description: Triggers when messages are received on the subscribed MQTT topic

outputs.description: Passes message information (topic, payload, qos, retain, timestamp_s, broker_host) to triggered jobs via run_config

broker_host.label: Broker Host
broker_host.description: MQTT broker hostname or IP address

topic.label: Topic
topic.description: MQTT topic to subscribe to. Supports wildcards: + (single level), # (multi-level)

job_name.label: Job Name
job_name.description: Name of the job to trigger when messages are received

qos.label: QoS Level
qos.description: MQTT Quality of Service level (0=at most once, 1=at least once, 2=exactly once)

collect_window_seconds.label: Collect Window (seconds)
collect_window_seconds.description: Seconds to listen for messages before disconnecting each evaluation

max_messages_per_poll.label: Max Messages Per Poll
max_messages_per_poll.description: Maximum number of messages to collect per evaluation

client_id.label: Client ID
client_id.description: MQTT client ID. Auto-generated if not set.

use_tls.label: Use TLS
use_tls.description: Whether to use TLS for the connection

username_env_var.label: Username Env Var
username_env_var.description: Name of the environment variable containing the MQTT username

password_env_var.label: Password Env Var
password_env_var.description: Name of the environment variable containing the MQTT password

resource_key.label: Resource Key
resource_key.description: Optional Dagster resource key providing a pre-configured client. When set, context.resources.<resource_key> is used instead of creating a connection from the other fields. See README for the expected interface.
